name: Mini-MBA

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: The four courses form a deliberate functional-breadth sampler covering accounting (ACCT 615), finance (FIN 600), information systems (MIS 645), and marketing (MRKT 632), which coheres as a mini-MBA survey for technical professionals. It notably omits a strategy/management and a leadership/organizational-behavior component that anchor most MBA cores, leaving the 'management' core of the credential thin.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a strategic-management or leadership/organizational-behavior course so the functional survey is anchored by an integrative management capstone.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: deepestPrereqChain is 0 and no course carries a prerequisite, which is appropriate and intentional for an open-entry breadth certificate aimed at professionals from any background; missingCourseCount=0 means no dangling references. The flat graph is a design feature here rather than a defect, though it means there is no sequencing.
dimensions.suggestion: Optionally publish a recommended order (foundations such as ACCT 615/FIN 600 before applied IT/marketing) even though hard prerequisites are unnecessary.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or objectives are present, so the page does not state what management competencies the mini-MBA develops.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish 3-5 program learning outcomes (e.g., interpret financial statements, evaluate a marketing strategy, assess IT for competitive advantage).

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: As a certificate, statedTotalCredits and creditReconcileDelta are null, but the design is cleanly balanced: exactly four uniform 3.0-credit courses, one per functional area, implying a focused 12-credit credential with all courses required. The only gap is that the total is not stated explicitly.
dimensions.suggestion: State the 12-credit total and that all four courses are required so the credit expectation is explicit.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: All four course descriptions are present and readable, and a four-course list is inherently legible, but degree is null with no program-level description, no stated credit total, and no plan grouping to frame the credential.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a brief program overview and a 12-credit total to the catalog page.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: The functional content is evergreen and marketable, with a fitting tech tilt for NJIT's engineering-management audience (MIS 645 on IT and competitive advantage, MRKT 632 on marketing for technology-based organizations), but the credential lacks contemporary management topics such as business analytics, digital transformation, and data-driven decision-making.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a course on business analytics for managers or digital transformation/AI in business to modernize the credential.

descriptionQuality.justification: All four descriptions are present, specific, and informative rather than placeholder: ACCT 615 lists concrete accounting topics, MIS 645 defines the information-systems field, and MRKT 632 frames technology marketing challenges.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: No prerequisites appear on any of the four courses, which is unambiguous and internally consistent with zero dangling references (missingCourseCount=0); the open-entry design is deliberate and clear, though it offers no sequencing to assess.

missingTopics.rationale: No dedicated strategy course anchors the functional sampler, leaving the integrative core of an MBA incomplete.
There is no course on managing people, teams, or organizational dynamics, a staple of MBA cores.
The four-course sampler omits operations, one of the core business functions.
MIS 645 covers IT broadly, but a hands-on analytics-for-managers course is absent.
No coverage of ethics, corporate governance, or ESG that a modern MBA-branded credential is expected to include.

emergingTrends.trend: AI and generative AI for business decision-making
emergingTrends.rationale: A mini-MBA for technical professionals should address how LLMs and AI reshape management, complementing MIS 645.

emergingTrends.trend: Digital transformation and platform business models
emergingTrends.rationale: MRKT 632 and MIS 645 could evolve to cover platformization and digital strategy explicitly.
Managers increasingly need analytics fluency; a short analytics module would modernize the credential.

emergingTrends.trend: Sustainability and ESG in business strategy
emergingTrends.rationale: ESG considerations are now central to corporate strategy and finance and warrant a place in the survey.
Technical professionals benefit from modern agile and product-management approaches to running teams and products.

topSuggestions: Add a strategic-management or leadership/organizational-behavior capstone to complete the MBA functional core, the biggest gap for an MBA-branded credential.
Publish 3-5 program-level learning outcomes for the certificate.
State the 12-credit total and the fixed four-course structure explicitly for transparency.
Add a business-analytics or AI-for-managers course to modernize the credential.
Introduce operations/supply-chain and ethics/ESG options to broaden functional coverage.
